text: This study examines the pivotal role of rare earth elements (REEs) in the global economy, focusing specifically on their application in high-performance permanent magnets. These technologies rely heavily on neodymium (Nd), praseodymium (Pr), dysprosium (Dy), and cerium (Ce), with a smaller dependence on gadolinium (Gd). Currently, the magnet industry represents the largest segment of the rare earth market by volume and is projected to maintain its dominance over other sectors. Drawing on market intelligence from Roskill and Wood Mackenzie, this report highlights forecasts indicating that magnet applications will constitute approximately 40% of total REE demand by 2030. Such significant growth suggests a looming supply-demand imbalance for critical magnetic elements. To address these challenges, this paper analyzes the current supply chains and market dynamics across the United States, Europe, Japan, and China.
